Name: pac_bayes_opt
Title: PAC-Bayes Optimization for Latency-Critical Machine Learning Inference
Short Hypothesis: PAC-Bayes frameworks can be used to derive strictly bounded-overhead protocols for latency-critical machine learning inference, enabling the optimization of model performance under strict latency constraints.
Related Work: Recent works such as SNIP, AMP-ViT, and EMQ have explored adaptive mixed-precision training and quantization methods for efficient model deployment. However, these approaches often rely on heuristic-based methods or require significant computational overhead. In contrast, our proposal leverages PAC-Bayes frameworks to provide a rigorous and efficient approach to optimizing model performance under latency constraints.
Abstract: We propose a novel approach to latency-critical machine learning inference using PAC-Bayes optimization. Our method derives strictly bounded-overhead protocols for model inference, enabling the optimization of model performance under strict latency constraints. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach through experiments on benchmark datasets, showcasing significant improvements in model performance while maintaining strict latency guarantees.
Experiments: Experiment 1: Evaluate the effectiveness of PAC-Bayes optimization on benchmark datasets (e.g., ImageNet) using standard latency-critical models (e.g., MobileNet)
Experiment 2: Compare the performance of our approach with state-of-the-art methods (e.g., SNIP, AMP-ViT) on latency-critical models
Experiment 3: Investigate the impact of different PAC-Bayes bounds on model performance and latency
Risk Factors and Limitations: The effectiveness of PAC-Bayes optimization may depend on the choice of bounds and hyperparameters
The approach may require significant computational resources for large-scale models
The method may not be applicable to all types of machine learning models or latency-critical applications
